biological_function.summary: TP53 encodes the tumor suppressor protein p53, often called the 'guardian of the genome.' It regulates the cell cycle and acts as a tumor suppressor by preventing genome mutation.

targeted_therapies.mechanism: Restores wild-type p53 function in mutant p53 tumors
CDK4/6 inhibitor; targets downstream of p53 pathway loss

literature.title: TP53 mutations in human cancers: origins, consequences, and clinical use
Mutant p53 in cancer: new functions and therapeutic opportunities

disclaimer: This tool is for research purposes only. Not for clinical use.
